zh.Cork, County Cork, Ireland: 愛爾蘭庫迪 科克科克
zh.Barrymore Castle, Cork, Cork, Ireland: 愛爾蘭庫迪 科克科克
zh.lifeSummary: Lord David Barry 6th Baron是David Barry 5th of Barry的兒子，於1337年出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 科克科克。他於大約1364年與Margaret de Courcy在愛爾蘭庫迪 科克科克結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1392年在愛爾蘭享年55歲。

zh-hans.Cork, County Cork, Ireland: 爱尔兰庫迪 科克科克
zh-hans.Barrymore Castle, Cork, Cork, Ireland: 爱尔兰庫迪 科克科克
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Lord David Barry 6th Baron是David Barry 5th of Barry的儿子，于1337年出生在爱尔兰庫迪 科克科克。他于大约1364年与Margaret de Courcy在爱尔兰庫迪 科克科克结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1392年在爱尔兰享年55岁。

nameMeanings.name: Barry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Donal, Aidan, Ciaran, Eoin, John Patrick, Kaitlin, Kieran, Murphy, Aileen, Brigid, Caitlin.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Beargha ‘descendant of Beargh’, a byname meaning ‘plunderer’.

Irish and Manx: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Báire ‘descendant of Báire’, a short form of either of two Gaelic personal names, Bairrfhionn or Fionnbharr, both meaning ‘fair-topped’. This may also be the source of the Manx name Barry, whose family's main seat was Ballavarry in Kirk Andreas.

variants.name: Barr
meanings: Scottish (Glasgow) and northern Irish (Ulster): habitational name from any of various places in southwestern Scotland, in particular Ayrshire and Renfrewshire, named with Gaelic barr ‘height, hill’ or a Brittonic cognate of this.

English and Welsh: habitational name from Great Barr in Staffordshire. This is from Brittonic barro- ‘top, summit’, referring to Barr Beacon; there is evidence that this was known as la Bare in the 13th century.
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Barre-en-Ouche in Eure, France, or perhaps from Barre-de-Semilly in Manche, France.

variants.name: Barrie
meanings: Scottish (Angus): habitational name from Barry in Angus or from another place so named, generally named with Gaelic barr ‘height, hill’, or from a British cognate of this word. This name is also the Scottish form of Barry .
French (Barrié): variant of Barrier .
Altered form of French   Baril  . Compare   Barry   7.

variants.name: Berry
meanings: Irish and Manx: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Béara ‘descendant of Béara’, a personal name of unexplained etymology; or, in some cases, perhaps an Anglicized form of Irish and Manx Ó Beargha. Compare Barry 1.
Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   Barrie  .
English: habitational name from any of several places called in Devon named with Old English byrig, dative case of burh ‘fortified manor house, stronghold’, such as Berry Pomeroy and Berrynarbor.

variants.name: Dubarry
meanings: French: variant of the topographic or habitational name Barry , with fused preposition and definite article du ‘from the’.

variants.name: Baril
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Normand, Alain, Armand, Ghislaine, Jacques, Jean-Marc, Jean-Pierre, Lucien, Monique, Phillippe.
French: from Old French baril ‘barrel’, applied as a metonymic occupational name for a cooper, or a nickname for a rotund man. Compare Barry 7 and Berry 7.

History: Jean Baril from the Saintonge region of France was in QC by 1673, when he married Marie Guillet, the first of his three wives. He died in 1724 in Batiscan, QC. His descendants also bear altered forms of the surname, such as Barrie, Bar(r)y, and Berry.

variants.name: Bary
meanings: Walloon and northern French: variant of   Barry   8.
French (southern): rare variant of   Barry   6.
Altered form of French   Baril  . Compare   Barry   7.

nameMeanings.name: Lord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of   Lorraine   or variant spelling of   Laurie .

variants.name: Lorraine
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, in origin denoting a migrant from the province of Lorraine in eastern France. This derives its name from Latin Lotharingia ‘territory of the people of Lothar’. The latter is a Germanic personal name derived from hlud ‘fame’ + heri, hari ‘army’. Lorraine began to be used as a girl's name in Scotland in the 19th century, and for a time in the second half of the 20th century enjoyed great popularity, which has since waned.

nameMeanings.name: David
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the greatest of all the kings of Israel, whose history is recounted with great vividness in the first and second books of Samuel and elsewhere. As a boy he killed the giant Philistine Goliath with his slingshot. As king of Judah, and later of all Israel, he expanded the power of the Israelites and established the security of their kingdom. He was also noted as a poet, many of the Psalms being attributed to him. The Hebrew derivation of the name is uncertain; it is said by some to represent a nursery word meaning ‘darling’. It is a very popular Jewish name, but is almost equally common among Gentiles in the English-speaking world. It is particularly common in Wales and Scotland, having been borne by the patron saint of Wales (  see  Dewi   ) and by two medieval kings of Scotland.

en.lifeSummary: Lord David Barry 6th Baron was born in 1337, in Cork, County Cork, Ireland as the son of David Barry 5th of Barry. He married Margaret de Courcy about 1364, in Cork, County Cork, Ireland. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1392, in Ireland, at the age of 55.

fr.lifeSummary: Lord David Barry 6th Baron est né en 1337, à Cork, County Cork, Irlande, fils de David Barry 5th of Barry. Il a épousé Margaret de Courcy environ 1364, à Cork, County Cork, Irlande. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1392, à Irlande, à l’âge de 55 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Lord David Barry 6th Baron wurde 1337 in Cork, County Cork, Irland, als Sohn von David Barry 5th of Barry geboren. Er hat um 1364 in Cork, County Cork, Irland Margaret de Courcy geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1392 im Alter von 55 Jahren in Irland.

it.lifeSummary: Lord David Barry 6th Baron è nato il nel 1337 nel Cork, County Cork, Irlanda come figlio di David Barry 5th of Barry. Lui sposato Margaret de Courcy circa 1364, nel Cork, County Cork, Irlanda. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1392, nel Irlanda, all'età di 55 anni.

ja.Cork, County Cork, Ireland: アイルランドコウントイ コルクコルク
ja.Barrymore Castle, Cork, Cork, Ireland: アイルランドコウントイ コルクコルク
ja.lifeSummary: Lord David Barry 6th Baronは1337年にアイルランドコウントイ コルクコルクでDavid Barry 5th of Barryの息子として生まれた。約1364年にアイルランドコウントイ コルクコルクでMargaret de Courcyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1392年にアイルランドで55歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lord David Barry 6th Baron(은)는 1337년 아일랜드 카운티 코르크 코르크에서 David Barry 5th of Barry의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 대략 1364년 아일랜드 카운티 코르크 코르크에서 Margaret de Courcy(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1392년 아일랜드에서 55세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Lord David Barry 6th Baron nasceu em 1337, em Cork, County Cork, Irlanda filho de David Barry 5th of Barry. Ele casou-se com Margaret de Courcy aproximadamente 1364, em Cork, County Cork, Irlanda. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1392, em Irlanda, com 55 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Lord David Barry 6th Baron родился в 1337 г. в местности корк, коунти корк, Ирландия. Его отца звали David Barry 5th of Barry. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret de Courcy около 1364 г. в местности корк, коунти корк, Ирландия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1392 г. в местности Ирландия в возрасте 55 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Lord David Barry 6th Baron nació en 1337, en Cork, County Cork, Irlanda como hijo de David Barry 5th of Barry. Se casó con Margaret de Courcy aproximadamente 1364, en Cork, County Cork, Irlanda. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1392, en Irlanda, a la edad de 55 años.
